entry.title: Re: Scripting Error in WebGUI for HTML

entry.title: Re: Set Default Filter Value for Work Center Category in...
entry.content:
hello nishtha,

It is not technically possible to set a default value for Work Center Category in the value help of the standard Fiori app F1511A. Even if enforced, defaulting to category 0005(plant maintenance) it could lead to mismatches with work centers used by other teams like Production. Therefore, it is not recommended from both a technical and business perspective.

Thanks &regards,
Neeraj.

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Language Layer
entry.content:
Hi bambi2913, 

Thank you for sharing your inquiry on the SAP Community! This topic should be addressed with our Product Support Team.  Please create a Product Support ticket using SAP for Me (log in with S-User ID and password), as described in this video, to have a product expert look into this specific issue. 

If you have additional questions in creating a support case, you can find more details in the SAP Signavio Onboarding Resource Center.  

All the best,

Dana

entry.title: Return PO Reference Linking and Quantity Validation
entry.content:
Hi Expert

In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, is there a field that displays the original purchase order number associated with a return purchase order? Additionally, when a return PO is created by copying from the original purchase order, is there a system control in place to ensure that the return quantity does not exceed the quantity of the original document?

在 S/4HANA Cloud Public 版本中，請問是否有欄位可以顯示與退貨採購單對應的原始正向採購單號？另外，若是從正向採購單複製建立退貨採購單，系統是否有數量不得超過原始單據的控管機制？

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits Series – Field Catalogs for Transportation Cockpit in SAP T
entry.content:

My new field is now available and editable. I’m not sure how to use READ_ONLY_REF, but I was able to make the field editable by modifying the READ_ONLY property in the GET_DEFINITION method of the feeder class.

I have another question regarding an existing field: how can we enable it as an input field? Currently, it appears as read-only (text view), and it doesn't seem possible to change this through customizing.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Pre-Upgrade Option for Release 2508
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"Independent of the upgrades of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment, we recommend developing automated tests for the custom-built apps to detect regressions early on and without manual effort for re-testing. Such automated tests are perfectly suited to be run in the pre-upgrade test phase."

 

Thanks for your sharing, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: Return PO Reference Linking and Quantity Validation
entry.content:
Dear Haily

There should a standard way to link a return purchase order to its original purchase order. Have you considered doing below verifications?

  1. Original Purchase Order Reference Field:
    When creating a return purchase order, especially via the "Copy" function, the system automatically captures the reference to the original purchase order.
    This reference is visible in the "Reference Document" field (usually available in the PO header or item level, depending on the app used like “Manage Purchase Orders”).
    The field may be labeled as "Reference PO" or appear under document flow or item reference data.

  2. System Control for Quantity Consistency:
    SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not automatically block users from entering a return quantity that exceeds the quantity of the original PO, unless additional custom logic or validations are implemented (via BAdI or SSCUI restrictions, if available).
    However, when returns are processed against goods receipt (e.g., through material documents), the system will compare the quantity of goods returned to the quantity received and prevent over-returning through standard stock checks and tolerance limits.

Recommendation:

  • To enforce strict control, leverage configurable tolerance limits or output controls.
  • Consider using apps like “Monitor Purchase Order Items” or “Manage Purchase Orders Centrally” to trace the document flow between the return PO and original PO.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition What’s New: Leave us your Feedback!
entry.content: I love the idea but I was wondering if you could make this whats new commenting function a bit more a collaboration tool for customers within their own organizations as well (kind of private comment)

entry.title: Entry of deleted function group still appears in SE80 and Eclipse
entry.content:
Hello,

I have deleted a function group but its name is still shown in Eclipse (it's the same in SE80):

If I delete it again, it says it's deleted. If I double click the name, it says it's deleted.

What can I do to remove it from the screen?

Sandra

System: ABAP 7.58 SP 1

entry.title: Re: Entry of deleted function group still appears in SE80...
entry.content:
The function group is probably linked to a modifiable transport request and has an Object Catalog Entry (e.g. R3TR FUGR).

The Object Catalog Entry needs to be deleted to remove the function group from the display.

To delete the Object Catalog Entry, either release the transport request or delete manually the Object Catalog Entry via SM30 > TADIR > R3TR FUGR function group name.

entry.title: Wage types not showing in Earnings in payslip
entry.content:
Hi team...I am using a sandbox system and the wage types for the pernr are not showing under earnings, all the wage types and taxes are showing under deductions , y are the wage types showing in deductions 

this is my RT



Could anyone please help??

entry.title: Re: Customer direct transfer approach with contact person
entry.content:
Yes, it is possible to migrate a customer with a contact person using internal number assignment in one go.

Please check the customization settings below:

  1. Business group mapped to customer account group
    SPRO Path: Cross-Application Components → Master Data Synchronization → Customer/Vendor Integration → Business Partner Settings → Field Assignment for Customer Integration → Assign Keys → Define Number Assignment for Direction Customer to BP

  2. Number range for Business Partner
    SPRO Path: Cross-Application Components → Master Data Synchronization → SAP Business Partner → Business Partner → Basic Settings → Number Ranges and Groupings → Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges

  3. Number range for Customer
    SPRO Path: Financial Accounting → Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable → Customer Accounts → Master Data → Preparations for Creating Customer Master Data → Assign Number Ranges to Customer Account Groups

Example:
If you maintain the mapping of the customer account group to the business partner grouping with the setting ‘SAME NO’ = ‘Yes’, it means the customer and business partner will be created with the same number.

In that case, check the number range settings of both customer and business partner to ensure there is no conflict between them.

Note:
The contact person and its related business partner are always created with internal number assignment using a grouping marked as the standard grouping.

Incase if the issue not resolved , then raise the incident with SAP in the component - CA-DT-MIG-S4

Regards,

Arun

entry.title: Re: Display Time Series Data in SAC
entry.content:
Hi Maike,

I understand using timeseries or Line chart would restrict the data to only 500 rows.
However, I found a small workaround that might help you with your analysis. 

Try using Bar/Column visualization and then set the top N to '9999' rows which is the max limit. It will then at least help you preview 9999 columns of data in the chart.

With regards,
Siva Prakash

entry.title: Introducing the Success Guide: Empowering Smooth SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementations
entry.content:
In the world of enterprise transformation, implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is a strategic move toward agility and innovation. But with innovation comes complexity - and that’s where the Success Guide from SAP PSCC Cloud Customer Care makes a real difference.

📘What Is the Success Guide?

The Success Guide is a practical, experience-based resource shared by SAP P&E Solution Architects. It captures real lessons from global SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition projects, helping implementation teams identify potential risks early, avoid common pitfalls, and stay aligned with SAP’s Best Practices.

Unlike traditional documentation, the Success Guide is:

  • Concise and structured
  • Scenario-driven
  • Rich with practical recommendations
  • Continuously updated

It addresses critical areas like:

  • Project methodology and mindset
  • Data migration strategy
  • Configuration guidance
  • Upgrade coordination
  • Line-of-business-specific insights (Finance, Sales, Manufacturing, etc.)

🔁Updated Quarterly, Aligned with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Major Releases

The Success Guide is released in sync with major versions of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, ensuring relevance to the most recent system capabilities and restrictions (currently version 2502).

To ensure continuous accuracy and value, the content is reviewed and updated every quarter by SAP PSCC P&E Solution Architects, incorporating new insights, updated features, and evolving customer needs.

🎯Why It Matters

A successful SAP Cloud ERP project hinges on planning, execution, and learning from others’ experiences. The Success Guide directly supports this by:

✅Preventing costly missteps before go-live
✅Encouraging a true cloud mindset and Fit-to-Standard adoption
✅Promoting early, realistic data migration planning
✅Providing guidance for irreversible configurations
✅Helping align scope, organization structures, and upgrade timing

🧭 Who Should Use It?

The Success Guide is intended for:

  • SAP implementation project managers
  • Functional and technical consultants
  • Customer IT leads and internal SAP teams
  • Partners delivering SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solutions

It’s especially relevant during the Explore, Realize, and Deploy phases of SAP Activate methodology.

🌍Where to Access It?

The latest PDF version is published in the SAP Digital Library (Preview / Download) and curated via Microsoft Lists on the SAP PSCC Cloud Customer Care SharePoint. Each version includes a changelog and new entries based on direct feedback and regional insights.

💡Final Recommendation

Success starts with preparation—make the Success Guide part of yours. Before kicking off any SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition project, make sure your team reads the Success Guide. It’s an essential companion for navigating implementation complexity with confidence. For any feedback or suggestions, feel free to contact Ecco Liu directly at ecco.liu@sap.com.

Thank you for your attention and support.

entry.title: 《Success Guide》：助力 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 项目顺利实施的实战宝典
entry.content:
在企业数字化转型的征程中，实施 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 是一项面向未来的、具有前瞻思维的选择，它代表着向敏捷性与创新迈进的重要一步。但创新往往伴随复杂性，这正是《Success Guide（成功指南）》能够为项目实施带来真正帮助的原因。

Success Guide 是什么？

《Success Guide》由SAP PSCC产品成功团队的解决方案架构师 （P&E Solution Architects）们基于支持到的全球 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 实施项目的真实经验编写，内容聚焦项目实践与落地，旨在帮助项目团队提前识别潜在风险、规避常见陷阱，并始终保持与 SAP 最佳实践的一致性。

它不同于传统的文档说明，更加：

  • 简明扼要、结构清晰
  • 基于场景、直击问题
  • 富含实用建议和应对措施
  • 持续更新、紧跟版本节奏

涵盖的关键主题包括但不限于：

  • 项目方法论与云思维
  • 数据迁移策略
  • 配置阶段的注意事项
  • 升级节奏与项目排期协调
  • 各业务模块的实践要点（如财务、销售、制造等）

🔁每季度更新，紧跟 SAP 主版本节奏

Success Guide 与 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 的主版本（如当前版本 2502）保持同步更新，确保内容始终贴合最新的系统能力与限制。

为保障持续的实用性与准确性，Success Guide 的内容由 SAP PSCC 各区域的解决方案架构师（P&E Solution Architects）们每季度定期审阅与更新，持续吸收项目一线反馈、新增功能特性与客户需求。

🎯为什么 Success Guide 至关重要？

一场成功的 SAP ERP 公有云项目，离不开周密的规划、有序的执行，以及借鉴他人经验的能力。《Success Guide》正是这样一个赋能工具，它帮助您：

✅在上线前预防代价高昂的问题
✅推动团队真正建立起“云思维”与 Fit-to-Standard 实施理念
✅尽早制定并测试数据迁移计划，避免临近上线时“踩雷”
✅明确哪些配置是不可逆的，慎重处理关键决策
✅帮助你合理安排范围、组织结构设计与升级时间窗口

🧭 谁应该阅读 Success Guide？

Success Guide 适用于以下群体：

  • SAP 项目实施经理
  • 业务顾问与技术顾问
  • 客户方 IT 负责人或内部 SAP 团队成员
  • 负责交付 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 的合作伙伴

它特别适用于 SAP Activate 方法论中的 Explore（探索）、Realize（实现）、Deploy（部署）阶段。

🌍如何获取 Success Guide？

最新版 Success Guide 的 PDF 可在 SAP Digital Library (预览 / 下载) 获取，同时也由 SAP PSCC 团队在其Cloud Customer Care专题的SharePoint - Microsoft Lists 中进行维护和整理，每个版本都包含更新日志和新增条目，确保可追溯性。

💡最后建议

成功源于准备。《Success Guide》正是每个 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 项目团队在启动前值得阅读的参考资料。

项目复杂性无需独自应对，愿 Success Guide 能为您保驾护航。

如有任何意见或建议，欢迎直接联系 Ecco Liu（ecco.liu@sap.com）。

感谢您的关注与支持！

entry.title: Re: Planning for Work Centers using PPDS

Hi Ndubuisi,

We tried the 1st solution of splitting the activities, but in that what it does is tries to run the setup of 10 different orders back to back as it is not directly linked to the 2nd operation. is there a way to make sure that once the setup is done it mandatorily looks at the machine activity instead of picking up setup of another order as it considers the labour is free.?

Thanks,

Pruthvee

entry.title: Re: How to Resolve Issues Transporting Flavors - S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Hi, 

Adapt UI / SAP Screen Personas changes are applied for unique control IDs. If control ID is not appearing on the screen then changes are ignored. However, there are situations when control IDs are dynamic (some subscreen IDs are changed depending on selected TAB in Tabstrip, or Collapsible area is expanded/collapsed).

First of all, open the same application in the same state on Original Screen and then apply the Flavor on DEV and TEST. 
I assume you are talking about MIGO_GR classic application, so choose the same Tranasaction, Reference Document fields and then set the same state for Header data and Detail data collapsible areas.

If Flavors look the same now, then check the following documentation link. Looks like MIGO_GR has the same issue with dynamic ID as MIGO application. So you can try search MIGO in Control ID overwrites and dublicate the same entry for MIGO_GR. 

After that, you can create new Flavor, or update existing one:

  • Select new overwrite rule
  • Choose Menu -> Control Overwrites -> Update Flavors
  • Search for Flavor in Quick Search below
  • Select Flavor and Rule 
  • Push Apply

Now you have to think about the state of collapsible areas when you are opening Flavor. For that, you have to prepare onLoad event script which will expand both, Header and Details collapsible areas. Maybe also profile Tranasaction, Reference Document fields.

Transport Flavor and Control ID Overwrite rule to TEST system via Software Collection and check.

Something like that. The idea should be clear now.

Best Regards!

entry.title: How to move data from TEVEN to RT table in Payroll?
entry.content:
hello Expert , I am abap developer

I'm working on Payroll schema, and I want to include time data from TEVEN table (e.g. attendance or absence hours) into the RT (Results Table).

I know TEVEN is processed in Time Evaluation (TM00), but is there a way to transfer certain TEVEN data (like total hours) into Payroll RT using schema logic or a custom operation?

Regards,
Sunil Singireddy.

entry.title: Re: Sales Reporting - Orders Coming and Dispatched %
entry.content:
Hello Adam,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, there is no standard out-of-the-box report explicitly showing sales order lines with their completion dates and metrics like percentage completed next day or average completion time directly. However, you can achieve this analysis by combining several tools and approaches:

Options for Sales Order Completion Analysis

1. Sales Order Fulfillment Issues App
This app helps identify and monitor sales order fulfillment issues in real time, which can give insights into the status of sales orders and their completion progress. It allows you to track orders and resolve issues but does not directly provide completion time statistics or percentages completed next day.

2. Custom Analytical Queries and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
You can create custom analytical queries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to extract sales order line data, including custom fields related to order status or completion. Then, by integrating with SAP Analytics Cloud, you can build stories and dashboards to analyze completion times, calculate percentages completed within certain time frames (e.g., next day), and compute average completion durations.

3. Sales Analysis Content Package
SAP provides standard sales analysis content for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud that covers sales volumes, incoming orders, and billing data. While this content focuses more on volumes, margins, and conditions, it can be extended or combined with custom fields to analyze completion metrics if you capture completion dates in your sales order data.

4. CDS Views or Custom Reports
You can develop custom CDS views or reports to extract sales order lines with their creation and completion timestamps, then calculate the percentage completed the next day and average completion time. This requires development effort and appropriate authorizations.

Summary

  • There is no direct standard report showing sales order line completion percentages and average completion time in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

  • Use the Sales Order Fulfillment Issues app for monitoring order status and issues.

  • Create custom analytical queries and leverage SAP Analytics Cloud to build detailed reports and dashboards for completion time analysis.

  • Consider custom CDS views or reports for tailored metrics like % completed next day and average completion time.

This approach allows you to analyze sales order completion performance effectively by combining SAP standard tools with custom analytics

Best regards,
Patryk Walędziak

entry.title: Re: How to move data from TEVEN to RT table in Payroll?
entry.content:
Dear Taher

 In SAP HCM, it should be technically possible to transfer time data from TEVEN (which contains time events such as attendances or absences) into the RT (Results Table) during Payroll schema processing, but it requires indirect integration through Time Evaluation and possibly custom operations or PCRs. Here is how you could approach this:

1. Understand the Separation of Time Evaluation and Payroll

  • The TEVEN table is part of Time Management, and its data is used in Time Evaluation (e.g., with schema TM00).
  • Payroll does not directly read from TEVEN.
  • To bring TEVEN-based time data into Payroll, it must first be evaluated and stored in time wage types (in the ZL table or TIP), then passed to Payroll.

2. Use Time Evaluation to Generate Time Wage Types

  • In your Time Evaluation schema (e.g., TM00😞
    • Use PCRs to read TEVEN records.
    • Create corresponding time wage types (e.g., based on attendance or absence hours).
    • These wage types are stored in ZL (Time Wage Types) or C1 cluster.

3. Transfer Time Wage Types into Payroll

  • In the Payroll schema, use the function P2011 to read time data.
  • Use function TIME (or P2011) to import ZL entries into payroll.
  • You can then process those entries via PCRs to:
    • Convert them into earning/deduction wage types.
    • Move them into the RT (Results Table).

4. Optional: Custom Operation or Rule Logic

If you need to bring specific TEVEN info directly (bypassing full Time Evaluation), you can:

  • Write a custom operation (coded in ABAP, using RP-IMP-C2-* macros) to read TEVEN entries.
  • However, this is not standard practice and should be carefully evaluated for performance and consistency.

5. Best Practice

The recommended and standard approach is:

  • Use Time Evaluation (TM00) to transform TEVEN data into ZL time wage types.
  • Pass ZL to Payroll.
  • Use Payroll rules to process ZL and post results into RT.

Example Flow:

  1. TEVEN → Processed in TM00 → Generates ZL (e.g., wage type 9xxx for 8 hours).
  2. Payroll schema → Function TIME or P2011 → Reads ZL.
  3. PCR in Payroll → Maps 9xxx to WT 1xxx → Adds to RT.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Step 8 Summary, "Comments" in Contract terms document...
entry.content:
Hi

In SAP Ariba Contracts, the "Comments – Entire Contract Request" section under Step 8: Summary allows users to enter general comments related to the entire contract request.

Purpose of this field:

  • It is designed for high-level notes, remarks, or clarifications that apply to the overall contract request, rather than to a specific clause or attachment.
  • Typically used by the contract requester, contract manager, or approvers to provide context, instructions, or observations during the contract lifecycle.

Where these comments are shown:

  1. Contract Workspace overview page:

    • After the contract is created, the comment may be visible in the summary or detail view of the Contract Workspace.
    • Depending on system configuration, it may appear in a "Comments" or "History" section tied to the contract header.

  2. Audit Trail / History log:

    • These comments are often captured in the event history or approval history, allowing auditability of what was entered and when.

  3. Approval Workflow:

    • If configured, these comments can be shown to approvers in their task views, helping them understand the intent or background of the contract request before approving.

  4. Reports and dashboards (optional):

    • In some deployments, administrators or sourcing teams configure custom reports that include these comments for tracking or compliance reviews.

 

  • These comments are not part of the legal contract text or the main contract terms document.
  • They are also not pushed to ERP systems like SAP S/4HANA unless specifically integrated.
  • Visibility may depend on permissions and workflow role settings.

 

Use this field for short, structured input that gives internal stakeholders better visibility into the purpose, urgency, or risk considerations of the contract request.

Best regards

entry.title: Inventory Opening Balance DTW to be imported as DRAFT
entry.content:
Hi guys,
accordingly with KB Note 3378584 I'm trying  to execute the DTW but I would to import in DRAFT mode.

The note doesn't mention anything about and the XLT model OIQI-InventoryOpeningBalance (neither the child file) doesn't have any column referencing to DRAFT where to set tYES/tNO for DRAFT mode.

Please, have you any idea if is it possible or not?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Error Message AC166 during OAYZ configuration
entry.content: What did you try 'Area xx is not a real area'

entry.title: Re: How to delete unused ICF services in EWA
entry.content:
Dear Hirotaka have you considered using 

 transaction SICF?

Here is a way to disable unused ICF services:

1. Use transaction SICF

  • Go to transaction SICF.
  • Navigate through the ICF service hierarchy to locate the unused service reported by EWA.
  • You can use the search function (Ctrl+F) or filter by service name, path, or technical name.

2. Deactivate the ICF Service

  • Right-click the ICF service.
  • Select Deactivate Service.
  • Confirm the deactivation when prompted.

Once deactivated:

  • The service will no longer respond to HTTP requests.
  • It will be marked as inactive in SICF.
  • It won’t be available to end-users or external systems until reactivated.

3. Optional – Document the Change

  • Make a note or use Solution Manager Change Documentation to track why the service was disabled, especially if managed in a regulated environment.

4. Important Considerations

  • Only disable services that are confirmed to be unused, either by EWA monitoring over time or by internal review.
  • Do not deactivate services that might be called infrequently or by background jobs unless you're absolutely sure they are not needed.
  • You can reactivate the service at any time by right-clicking and choosing Activate Service.

5. Security Note

  • Disabling unused ICF services improves your system’s security posture by reducing the attack surface.
  • Consider regularly reviewing ICF services via EWA or Security Baseline Reporting.

Best regards

entry.title: Fixing Contingent Worker Misclassification in SAP SuccessFactors: A Step-by-Step Resolution
entry.content:
*Note: The scenario discussed is generalized and anonymized to respect client confidentiality.*

Section

H2 Heading

Context

## Introduction

Problem

## The Problem: Contingent Workers Consuming FTE Licenses

Impact

## Impact Analysis

Solution

## How We Solved It: Step-by-Step Fix

Best Practice

## Best Practice: Hiring a Contingent Worker the Right Way

Takeaways

## Lessons Learned

Wrap-up

## Conclusion

Engagement

## Share your insights in the comments

 

##Introduction

Contingent workers are increasingly a part of modern workforce strategy. SAP SuccessFactors supports their unique lifecycle with dedicated functionality distinct from that of full-time employees (FTEs). However, incorrect configuration can lead to misclassification, which in turn results in license over-utilization, flawed reports, and compliance risks.

In this blog, I share a real client scenario where contingent workers were unintentionally configured as FTEs. I outline the challenges we faced, the impact, and the step-by-step remediation strategy based on SAP best practices.


##The Problem: Contingent Workers Consuming FTE License

At a client site, contingent workers were mistakenly on-boarded using FTE-specific event reasons such as "Hire - New Hire" and "Hire - Replacement". As a result:

  • FTE licenses were consumed inadvertently
  • Organizational headcount and cost center reports were inflated
  • Workers remained in "active" status post separation

The issue stemmed from a lack of clarity on the correct process for Onboarding contingent workers in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central.

 

##Impact Analysis

The misconfiguration had significant downstream effects:

  • License Over-utilization

              Contingent workers consumed paid FTE licenses unnecessarily.

  • Data Inaccuracy

               Reports on turnover, cost allocations, and active headcount were unreliable.

  • Separation Failures

              Due to incorrect event types, the system did not terminate contingent workers as expected, leaving them in active status.

 

##How We Solved It: Step-by-Step Fix

Step 1: Extract Job Information

  • Go to: Reporting > All Reports
  • Run and export the Job Info report for all affected workers.

Step 2: Prepare the Corrected Job History Template

  • Navigate to Import Employee Data.
  • Download the Job Information Template
  • Update the event reasons to reflect contingent worker actions (e.g., "Start CWK" for hire, and appropriate contingent termination reasons)

  • Ensure the event sequence is chronological

Step 3: Import the Corrected Job History

  • Select: Import Employee Data > Import Data
  • Choose Job History as the entity
  • Select Full Purge and upload the corrected file

  • Validate results in test system before deploying in production

This process ensured proper categorization of workers and restored data accuracy and license compliance.

 

##Best Practice: Hiring a Contingent Worker the Right Way

Step 1: Use the “Add Contingent Worker” Function

  • Select this dedicated option to avoid FTE misclassification

Step 2: Complete Personal and Assignment Information

  • Use "Start CWK" as the event reason

  • Fill in both the Assignment Info and Work Order sections with contract end dates

  • Assign correct Position and Work Order Name

Step 3: Submit

  • Review all data
  • Click Submit to finalize and ensure the worker is correctly classified.

When done correctly, this ensures that the worker is tagged as a contingent worker and does not consume an FTE license.


##Lessons Learned

  • Always align event reasons with the correct worker type (FTE vs. CWK)
  • Collaborate between HR and IT to verify template usage and hiring flow
  • Running regular audits on employee types and license assignments

 

##Conclusion

Misconfiguring contingent workers as full-time employees can lead to significant business and compliance risks. By following SAP’s best practices for worker classification, organizations can avoid these pitfalls while ensuring clean, accurate data and optimized license usage.

 

##Share your insights in the comment

Have you encountered similar issues in your implementation? What strategies worked for you? Share your insights into the comments — let's build a smarter, more compliant SuccessFactors community together.

entry.title: How to transfer only data from SF Production to Test environment
entry.content:
Hello,

our customer wants to only transfer data, not configuration from their SF Production to Test environment. Is there a way to do it without a full Instance Refresh because currently some configurations are only existing in Test and should not be overwritten by a full transfer.

I read about the Configuration Transport Center but this functionality is only to transfer configuration, not data.

So our goal is to transfer only all current data from all the modules used except LMS, such as Job Requisitions and Candidates, Performance Forms, Development Data and Employee Central data. 

Is there a way to do this?

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM transport release and deployment issue
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Would like to know how transport movement work with SAP Cloud ALM - Features. We have project created and STMS from S/4 HANA private cloud systems connected and all setup completed. 

I expect that changing a Feature status from "Implementation" to "Test" will also release any Transport Requests in the Feauture and move them to other system, like in SAP Solution Manager ChaRM Change Documents. But Features don't do this. I change the status of the Feature, but nothing happens to the Transport Requests.

I tried to manually release Transport Requests, and I'm getting some error. The same when I try to release the Transport Request. When I deploy, i can't find the Transport Request anywhere in S/4 HANA system.

Can you please explain how the Transport management works with CALM? I think transport release is not working in our case. Please help. 

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM transport release and deployment issue
entry.content:
The SAP Help Portal SAP Cloud ALM - Application Help has detailed sections about:

  • Working with Features
  • Working with Transports
  • Deploying Features

SAP Cloud ALM Features do not function identically to SAP Solution Manager ChaRM Change Documents. The release and import of Transport Requests is not tied to the same action for changing the Feature status. Instead, transport actions must be triggered using the relevant buttons in the "Transports" section of the Feature. This is not customizable or configurable, so the provided workflow of Features must be used.

A Transport Request can only be released from the Feature UI if all of its child Transport Tasks have already been released. If the Transport Tasks are not released, then any attempt to release the Transport Request will fail. 

There can be multiple reasons why a Transport Request release is failing. Please refer to KBA 3398300 "Transports/Transport of Copies not created/released in On-premise Managed system from SAP Cloud ALM Feature" for more in-depth guidance.

There can also be multiple reasons why a Transport Request deployment (import) is failing. Please refer to KBA 3552453 "Transports not deployed/imported in On-premise Managed system from SAP Cloud ALM Feature" for more in-depth guidance.

entry.title: How to calculate Bonus based on a specific date other than template effective date or point-in-time?
entry.content:
This blog explains how to fetch data (effective) as of certain date from assignment level details and bring it up to summary row for further calculations.

Scenario:  Customer wants to have all the data that contributes to Bonus calculation to be based on (effective as of) a different date than the below two dates.

a.  Template effective date - Summary row (Entry level) data in Variable pay form is pulled in from Employee Central based on the template effective date.

b. Effective date for Point-in-time calculation - Basis amount is calculated based on the effective date specified. 

Solution:

Step 1: Add these 2 fields to the assignment level to import the startDate and endDate from Employee History File.     

 

 Step 2: Add a field to hold the Bonus cut-off date (the date which you want to fetch the data for bonus calculations) in Entry level for ease of maintenance

Step 3: Add these 2 fields to the assignment level to calculate the difference between startDate and Bonus cut-off date & endDate and Bonus cut-off date for each assignment

 

Step 4: Add a field “Include Cutoff Date?” with the below formula in assignment level.

PS: This is how you find the Bonus-cut-off date falls under which employee history record

Logic: if start date <= 6/30/2023 AND end date >= 6/30/2023, then YES, else No 

Step 5: If YES, pull that salary and/or FTE etc. from assignment level and aggregate up to summary fields -: Last Business Day of Q3 Salary and Last Business Day of Q3 FTE

 

 

 Results:

entry.title: Re: need help with Electronic Bank Statement MT940, probl...
entry.content:
Hi 

 

In SAP Electronic Bank Statement (EBS) configuration using MT940, if your system is reading the amount incorrectly (e.g., 25,000,000 instead of 250,000), it usually means that the MT940 file uses an implicit decimal format, and your SAP system expects explicit decimals (or vice versa).

Problem Summary:

  • MT940 file contains: 25000000 (which is actually 250,000.00 IDR).
  • SAP interprets it as 25,000,000 IDR.
  • Your IDR currency is configured without decimal places in SAP (standard behavior for IDR).
  • You want SAP to treat the last two digits as decimals, effectively dividing by 100.

Solution Options:

Option 1: Use BADI FIEB_CHANGE_BS_DATA

This is the correct and most flexible way to handle amount conversion in EBS processing.

  1. Go to transaction SE18 or SE19 to implement enhancement spot FIEB_CHANGE_BS_DATA.

  2. In method IF_EX_FIEB_CHANGE_BS_DATA~CHANGE_LINE_DATA, write custom logic to divide the amount by 100 before it’s posted.

    Sample logic:

    IF i_fiebh-filetype = '940'.
  IF i_fiebh-curr = 'IDR'.
    c_fiebd-amount = c_fiebd-amount / 100.
  ENDIF.
ENDIF.

  3. Activate and test. This approach works at runtime, so no need to modify the MT940 file or currency settings.

Option 2: Adjust the MT940 file at the source (bank side)

If possible, ask the bank to format amounts in a way that matches SAP expectations.

For IDR (which has 0 decimals in SAP), the bank should send:

  • 250000 instead of 25000000 for 250,000 IDR.

But this requires coordination with the bank, which may not always be possible.

 

Important Note:

Do not try to modify the currency decimal definition for IDR in SAP. SAP defines IDR as a zero-decimal currency, and changing that breaks compliance with standard settings and may cause issues in financial reports, payment programs, and rounding.

Recommendation:

Use BADI FIEB_CHANGE_BS_DATA to apply the logic cleanly, safely, and without needing to alter the MT940 file or currency settings.

Best regards

1. Checked the Transaction and it has a tagged Primary Form Item.

2. Checked the Cash Account and it is set as Cash Flow Relevant with an appropriate Cash Flow Category.

3. Checked the Cash Flow Reference Report and the transaction has been assigned.

 Database is SAP HANA Version 10 PL 02

I have tried to execute a FUL settlement in KO88 and there is no quantity updated in CO document also. So what could be the reason for not updating the qty in CO document ?

I’m currently using the "Manage Operating Chart of Accounts" app, and I've noticed that it doesn't allow me to mass update the assignments of G/L accounts from the YCOA to different company codes. It seems I can only do it one by one, which is quite time-consuming.

Additionally, when I try to perform the action, I get an error. I’m attaching a screenshot of the error for reference.

Does anyone know if there is another app or tool available in SAP S/4HANA that allows for mass maintenance of these assignments?

Any help or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

Best regards,
Jorge

entry.title: Inquiry about Adapt UI on Dependent Fields in Customization Tenant with Master Data
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

We are currently preparing for a system go-live and following the standard 3-system landscape process. Our Customization Tenant (D/C system) is used for CBC configuration, IMG settings, business role maintenance, and custom report setups. All configurations and extensions are transported to the Test system using the Export Customizing Transports and Export Software Collection apps, and then forwarded to Production after testing.

 

However, we have a requirement to adjust some field labels using Adapt UI for the Project Control - Enterprise Projects Fiori app. The fields that need adaptation only appear after creating a project and selecting a specific project profile, meaning they are context-dependent and not always visible on the default screen.

 

Since we are advised trying not to create any master data in the D/C tenant, we are facing a challenge:

Adapt UI cannot be used unless we create sample master data first.

 

We found SAP Note 2680324 , which seems to suggest that UI adaptations rely on screen context and runtime data. Based on this, we would like to confirm:

  1. Is it acceptable and recommended to temporarily create example master data in the Customization Tenant solely for Adapt UI purposes, and delete them afterward?
  2. Is there an official best practice for handling this situation when the UI elements requiring adaptation are only visible after entering specific master data?
  3. Are there any alternative solutions available that would allow us to perform UI adaptation without creating test master data?

 

Best regards,
Tiffany

entry.title: SAP Build Apps: how do you mitigate the CORS issue when the app accesses another SAP BTP service?
entry.content:
I wonder what is the best method to solve the CORS issue specifically in the SAP Build Apps web app?

For example: I built an app which uses SAP AI Core. In the preview it works like charm, but when deployed, it throws the HTTP 403 error, which for sure is CORS-related.

What is the best method to solve this issue? Some properties in the BTP destination, defined in the Build Apps subaccount and leading to the SAP AI Core? Or any other method? Any ideas?

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP runtimes, my personal considerations and preferences on Cloud Foundry, Kyma, ABAP runtim
entry.content:
Thank you for this interesting information
I do not agree on your remark about isolation on ABAP
you're right that resource isolation is definitely less efficient/native on ABAP compared to CF or Kyma... however, the example taken is excessive: a (small) poorly coded program can't bring down an ABAP instance - many mechanisms exist to avoid this type of catastrophe.

entry.title: Data Privacy Integration NextGen: Elevating Privacy to New Heights
entry.content:
In the digital age, where every click and swipe leaves a trail, data privacy has moved from a back-room IT discussion to a front-page societal concern. Every day, whether we’re binge-watching a new series, ordering a pizza online, or just browsing memes, our personal information is being collected at a dizzying pace. It’s akin to navigating a vast digital city where every step we take is logged and monitored.

 It's like every corner of this digital landscape is dotted with invisible watchers taking notes. Creepy? A bit. Necessary? Absolutely.

Consumers are waking up to this reality, demanding greater control over their digital footprints and pushing companies to re-evaluate their approach to data privacy. 

The stakes? Astronomically high. From global corporations to government agencies, the recognition of data privacy as a fundamental right is growing, fueled by the understanding that our personal information is not just data points, but the very essence of our digital identities. 

Recognizing the pressing need of the moment, SAP introduces Data Privacy Integration NextGen - a Unified Data Privacy offering for Intelligent Enterprise. This robust solution consolidates all its previous data privacy solutions under one comprehensive umbrella. DPI NextGen is designed to support businesses in addressing the challenges in this crucial area. Keep in mind, the insights shared here are meant to inform and inspire, not serve as legal advice.

Why DPI NextGen?

A Unified Hub for All Your Needs

Think about your smartphone. It’s not just a phone; it’s a camera, a GPS, a music player, and so much more, all in one compact device. You don’t need separate gadgets for each function; everything is seamlessly integrated and readily accessible.

Now, let’s draw a parallel to DPI NextGen, which unifies all your data privacy capabilities in a single offering. Features like Information Reporting and Retention Management come together to streamline compliance and give users easy access to a comprehensive suite of privacy tools. Gone are the days of juggling multiple apps—DPI NextGen is your one-stop shop for all things data privacy!

Making Intelligent Enterprise a Reality

DPI NextGen embraces the vision of an Intelligent Enterprise by offering a flexible platform experience across SAP applications. You have the power to choose the technology stack and integrations with hyperscalers that align best with your organization’s unique needs. Effortlessly integrate with existing cloud solutions and watch as your operations undergo a transformative shift.

Improved Features

But wait—there’s more! DPI NextGen is not just about consolidation; it's enhanced by a treasure trove of impressive new features:

Automated Archival and Deletion: Say goodbye to manual intervention! Automation capabilities allow for scheduled archival or deletion of data, ensuring compliance without the headache.

Multilingual Support: Operate in 33 different languages! This feature enhances collaboration among global teams, breaking down language barriers and fostering a more inclusive workspace.

Experience DPI NextGen with Our Free Tier Plan

As we phase out older services, we are excited to offer a new free tier plan for DPI NextGen. Dive into our service with a fixed quota of complimentary requests. Allowing you to explore the capabilities of DPI NextGen without any commitment. Once you’re hooked, transitioning to our Enterprise plan is smooth and straightforward. The details of which can be found here.

While the pricing for DPI NextGen may be higher than that of DPI 1.0, it is important to highlight that the enhanced performance and features will provide substantial returns on your investment. Don’t miss this opportunity to elevate your data privacy strategy—try our free tier plan today and discover the difference for yourself!

Why Make the Switch to DPI NextGen?

Here are a few compelling reasons to consider migrating to our new offering:

  • Discontinuation of Older DPP Offerings: Our older Data Privacy Protection (DPP) offerings will be phased out soon, which means they will no longer be available. Transitioning now ensures you won’t be left without a solution.
  • Enhanced Flexibility and ROI with BTPEA: The new plan on BTPEA provides greater flexibility and agility, allowing you to leverage modern services on a consumption-based model. This approach not only improves your return on investment (ROI) but also helps facilitate your organization's modernization efforts.
  • Comprehensive Support for a Smooth Transition: Migration doesn’t have to be daunting. Our DPI NextGen team offers a step-by-step guide to ensure a seamless transition, along with complete support for migrating your data from older DPI offerings.

Sneak Peak: What’s coming next in DPI NextGen?

  • Easy orchestration of data deletion across all platforms helping several applications to align on the timing of data blocking ensuring all data is handled uniformly and at the same point in time. No more juggling!
  • We are broadening our capabilities to meet the diverse needs of our growing client base by deploying additional data centers, enhancing our global reach and performance.
  • Our upcoming improvements will allow for flexible information analysis, accommodating various data types to provide deeper insights and more effective data management.
  • Integrating with a leading authentication service to simplify and strengthen user access, ensuring a seamless and secure experience.
  • Easy to get started SAAS application with no manual prerequisites providing the best experience possible for our customers.

Conclusion

As we forge new paths in the ever-evolving digital landscape, the imperative for robust data privacy solutions has never been clearer. DPI NextGen rises to this challenge, offering a comprehensive suite of privacy tools that seamlessly blend simplicity with sophistication. This ensures that your enterprise is not only equipped to meet today’s demands but is also prepared to navigate the uncertainties of tomorrow.

DPI NextGen serves as a critical backbone for applications striving to achieve essential data privacy compliance. By consolidating all privacy functionalities into a single, unified platform, it represents a transformative leap forward that many users have eagerly anticipated.

Yet, to unlock the full potential of DPI NextGen, we must embrace widespread adoption and foster unwavering support from all stakeholders involved. It is our shared responsibility as users to champion and prioritize data privacy, while organizations must recognize and uphold the intrinsic value of protecting the sensitive information entrusted to them.

With ongoing innovations and a collective commitment, we can shape a future where data privacy is not just an ancillary concern but recognized as a fundamental right. Together, let us strive to create a digital world where trust, transparency, and respect for personal information form the cornerstone of every interaction. By doing so, we will ensure that privacy is not merely an aspiration but a reality for everyone.

Explore More

End User Guide

Developer Guide

What’s New

Security Guide

API Documentation

BTP Discovery Centre

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance: Create your SAP S/4HANA system in a fraction of the usual
entry.content:

Make Variant : 793_REL
Database Client Library : SQLDBC 2.24.021.1739564224
Created in : Linux GNU SLES-15 x86_64  cc10.3.0 use-pr250404
Created on : Apr 04 2025 21:45:21
Kernel Patch Level : 225

entry.title: Unable to add Northwind destination in SAP BUILD Apps integration
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I have a use case wherein I wish to create a Northwind destination in cockpit and add that destination as SAP system in SAP BUILD Apps.

 

Below are my destination details:

I am able to connect successfully from cockpit.

However, When I try to add this SAP system through Integrations tab , I get below error:

On Inspect, below are the details.

Please provide your valuable thoughts on this issue.

entry.title: Re: SAP BDC for Dummies
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"

SAP BDC is a fully managed SaaS platform that integrates:

  • SAP Datasphere for data modelling and integration
  • SAP Analytics Cloud for analytics and planning
  • SAP Business Warehouse capabilities (private cloud edition only)
  • Managed Databricks for advanced data processing (with limited features)
  • Context-aware intelligent applications for AI-driven insights (SAP Joule & Databrick's Mosaic)"

 

Thanks for your sharing, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: How to find the custom ABAP artifacts in the SAP system using the SAP tables.
entry.content:
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZVGTCUSTDEVLST'

@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true

@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'Get list of custom DDIC objects'

@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true

define view ZPBIST_UTIL_GET_CUST_DEV_LIST as

 

select from tadir

{

 

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Object Type Description'

case object when 'DDLS' then 'CDS Data Definition'

when 'TABL' then 'Database Table'

when 'DOMA' then 'Domain'

when 'DTEL' then 'Data Element'

when 'TTYP' then 'Table Type'

when 'CMOD' then 'Customer Exits'

when 'SXCI' then 'Badi Implementations'

when 'ENHO' then 'Implicit Enhancements'

when 'DEVC' then 'Development Package'

end as ObjectTypeDetails,

 

cast( object as ABAP.char( 50 ) ) as ObjectType,

cast( obj_name as ABAP.char( 50 ) ) as ObjectName,

cast( '' as ABAP.char( 50 ) ) as ReferenceObject,

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Development Package'

cast( devclass as abap.char( 50 ) ) as DevPackage

 

}

where pgmid = 'R3TR' and obj_name like 'Z%' and

( object = 'DDLS' or object = 'TABL' or object = 'DOMA' or object = 'DTEL' or object = 'TTYP' or

object = 'CMOD' or object = 'SXCI' or object = 'ENHO' or object = 'DEVC' )

and devclass <> '$TMP'

 

union all

 

select from dd02l

{

case tabclass when 'INTTAB' then 'Structure'

when 'APPEND' then 'Append Structure'

end as ObjectTypeDetails,

cast( tabclass as ABAP.char( 50 ) ) as ObjectType,

cast( tabname as ABAP.char( 50 ) ) as ObjectName,

cast( sqltab as ABAP.char( 50 ) ) as ReferenceObject,

cast( ' ' as abap.char( 50 ) ) as DevPackage

}

where tabname like 'Z%' and

( tabclass = 'INTTAB' or tabclass = 'APPEND' )

 

union all

 

select from dd25l

{

case aggtype when 'V' then 'Views'

when 'M' then 'Match Code Object'

when 'E' then 'Lock Object'

end as ObjectTypeDetails,

cast( aggtype as ABAP.char( 50 ) ) as ObjectType,

cast( viewname as ABAP.char( 50 ) ) as ObjectName,

cast( roottab as ABAP.char( 50 ) ) as ReferenceObject,

cast( ' ' as abap.char( 50 ) ) as DevPackage

}

where viewname like 'Z%' and

( aggtype = 'V' or aggtype = 'M' or aggtype = 'E' )

entry.title: Re: Error Message AC166 during OAYZ configuration
entry.content:
Was the entry someway deleted from T093, with transaction OADB 'Define Depreciation Areas' when existing records from ANKB are displayed in transaction OAYZ 'Determine Depreciation Areas in the Asset Class'.

  • Did you execute 'Check Consistency' (ANKA, OAK3 or OAK6)
  • Are there already some asset using this area
  • Did you try to recreate the area, save and then delete it again.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1 Release: Project Manufacturing Management and Optimization – What’s New
entry.content: thanks

entry.title: Re: Planning Calendar Integration into IBP
entry.content:
Hi manikandan_shanmugam.

Were you able to integrate the custom calendar in IBP? Would you mind sharing how you did it?

I've been reading that perhaps next year will have a fiori app for the creation ofn factory calendars in IBP. The integration seems to be a complex endeavour.

You would think that being both SAP systems it would be much easier.

Look forward for comments

entry.title: Check lot create and Print
entry.content:
Hello team,

 

Business wants to have check lot create and check print in the system.

 

we are having a business requirement wherein check lot creation and print is required.

 

kindly provide us the solution as we are not able to create any check lot,

 

Is there any scope ID activation is necessary for the same?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Check lot create and Print
entry.content:
See 

Checks

Configuring the Output of Outgoing Checks

See also kba 3605460 - No check lot could be determined

Note for apps in the fiori library you can see related scope items 

example app Manage Checkbooks 

SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library

here you can see 


Scope Items
The following scope items in the SAP Best Practices are using this app.

Scope Item Group   Scope Item
Finance                 Accounts Payable (J60)

Finance                 Accounts Receivable (J59)

Finance                 Bill of Exchange (1WQ)

entry.title: Re: SUNAT Withholding Agents in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public...
entry.content:
Hola Luisa,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there is currently no standard mass upload tool or SSCUI specifically designed for updating SUNAT withholding agent data. This maintenance is typically done manually via the "Manage Business Partner" app, where withholding agent status is maintained per supplier.

However, you can consider these approaches:

  • Using the Business Partner API (API_BUSINESS_PARTNER) to automate updates from the SUNAT file via integration or middleware.
  • SAP BTP Extension for parsing SUNAT updates and posting to S/4HANA Cloud using standard APIs.
  • Regularly consult the SAP Localisation Toolkit for Peru, as enhancements are ongoing.

As of now, there’s no dedicated SAP Note for this functionality, but I’d recommend following the SAP Notes below:

SAP Note 3090071 for Peru localisation updates.

SAP Note 3378240 - S/4HANA Country specific localization guides

Hope this helps!

Best regards,

Chuma

entry.title: Re: FI/AP Arquivo retorno de pagamento a fornecedor SAP FI
entry.content: Telma, bom dia.

entry.title: Re: FI/AP Arquivo retorno de pagamento a fornecedor SAP FI

entry.title: Re: Dunning Notice in SAP Best Practice from ABAPer prospective
entry.content:
Thank you.

Just one thing: for the email subject you have to fill the field C_ITCPO-TDTITLE and not C_ITCPO-TDCOVTITLE.

entry.content: Is it planned to apply the note 3321845 in EWM 9.5? Is there any chance to have the same functionality in EWM 9.5?

entry.content: update on 06/19/2025, issue resolved.

entry.title: Re: Error Message AC166 during OAYZ configuration
entry.content: We deleted Area 15 as was not required and we deleted thorugh OADB depreciation areas.. for one particular asset class we are facing the issue in OAYZ. There is no asset created for that asset class. We need to activate required depreciation area.

entry.title: How to get material number from KSSK table
entry.content:
Hi SAP Gurus ,

I’m trying to get a list of all SKUS in the system that has a specific batch class assigned to it . To do this I did the below :

  • Went to table KLAH and found the  value of CLINT (In class no) against the KLART ( Class Type)
  • Went into the table KSSK and entered and ran it by entering the required CLINT (In class no)

The table gave me a list of object numbers which I thought are the Material numbers(looking at some of the information on other threads)  with preceding with 0 but this doesn’t seem to be case the Object numbers seem to be system generated numbers sequential numbers .

 

Is there anyway to get all the Material Numbers that have a specific class within a class type assigned to it ?

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP Business Data Cloud - S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
entry.content: Great article Shailu with detailed steps to help our customers and partners get started with BDC
